categories.title: Visual System
categories.description: Strategies for visual direction, layout, typography, color, frameworks, and brand constraints.

categories.title: Motion System
categories.description: Strategies for purposeful, accessible, and performant interface animation.

capabilities.title: Coherent Interface
capabilities.description: Create a coherent and reviewable interface system.

capabilities.title: Purposeful Motion
capabilities.description: Create motion that communicates state and hierarchy.

constraints.title: Brand Constraints
constraints.description: Preserve approved brand tokens, voice, and visual restrictions.

strategies.title: High-Contrast Blocks
strategies.description: Use strong outlines, direct color blocks, and visible hierarchy without ornamental ambiguity.

evidence.summary: Useful when direct hierarchy and a deliberately bold visual voice match the brief.

strategies.title: Soft Depth
strategies.description: Use restrained tonal surfaces and shadows to imply depth while preserving contrast.

evidence.summary: Use only when depth cues remain clear under accessibility review.

strategies.title: Translucent Layers
strategies.description: Use transparent surfaces sparingly with explicit fallback contrast and hierarchy.

evidence.summary: Use where layered context is valuable and fallback readability is verified.

strategies.title: Editorial Minimal
strategies.description: Prioritize content rhythm, whitespace, hierarchy, and restrained visual elements.

evidence.summary: Useful when reading flow and content hierarchy dominate the experience.

strategies.title: Dense Data Interface
strategies.description: Organize high information density with clear grouping, scanning, and progressive detail.

evidence.summary: Useful for expert workflows that require comparison and rapid scanning.

strategies.title: Warm Human Interface
strategies.description: Use approachable language, rounded forms, and restrained warmth within brand constraints.

evidence.summary: Useful when reassurance and approachability are explicit audience needs.

strategies.title: Monochrome System
strategies.description: Build hierarchy primarily through value, spacing, typography, and limited accent color.

evidence.summary: Useful when restraint and content emphasis fit the visual brief.

strategies.title: Expressive Typography
strategies.description: Use a distinctive display type direction while preserving readable body and control text.

evidence.summary: Useful when typography is an explicit part of the visual identity.

strategies.title: Functional Type Scale
strategies.description: Define a bounded type scale mapped to content and interface roles.

evidence.summary: Apply when consistent reading hierarchy is required across surfaces.

strategies.title: Semantic Color
strategies.description: Map color tokens to stable roles and verify meaning without color alone.

evidence.summary: Apply to systems requiring reusable states and themes.

strategies.title: Gradient Backgrounds
strategies.description: Use bounded gradients as hierarchy or atmosphere while preserving content contrast.

evidence.summary: Use when atmospheric depth fits the brand and readability remains verified.

strategies.title: Textured Backgrounds
strategies.description: Use subtle original texture with performance and readability constraints.

evidence.summary: Use when tactile character is required and content remains clear.

strategies.title: Grid Layout
strategies.description: Use explicit columns, spacing, and alignment rules to organize interface structure.

evidence.summary: Apply when repeated alignment and predictable composition improve clarity.

strategies.title: Asymmetric Layout
strategies.description: Use controlled imbalance and varied rhythm while maintaining navigation clarity.

evidence.summary: Useful when a dynamic composition supports the content and brand.

strategies.title: Responsive Content
strategies.description: Adapt content priority and layout to available space rather than only resizing components.

evidence.summary: Apply to interfaces used across varied viewport sizes.

strategies.title: Mobile-First Layout
strategies.description: Establish essential content and interactions at constrained sizes before expansion.

evidence.summary: Useful when constrained-screen use is a primary product requirement.

strategies.title: Accessible Contrast
strategies.description: Verify text, controls, focus, states, and layered surfaces under accessibility criteria.

evidence.summary: Apply to every user-facing interface.

strategies.title: Focus-Visible Interactions
strategies.description: Design explicit keyboard focus, states, targets, and interaction feedback.

evidence.summary: Apply to interactive interfaces requiring keyboard accessibility.

strategies.title: shadcn/ui Composition
strategies.description: Compose owned component code from accessible primitives with project-specific styling.

evidence.summary: Select when source-owned component composition matches the project.

strategies.title: Radix UI Primitives
strategies.description: Build styled components on behavior-focused accessible primitives.

evidence.summary: Select when primitive behavior and custom styling match the project.

strategies.title: Material Components
strategies.description: Use a Material component system with explicit theming and brand review.

evidence.summary: Select when Material interaction and component conventions fit product constraints.

strategies.title: Bootstrap Components
strategies.description: Use Bootstrap layout and components with deliberate token and accessibility review.

evidence.summary: Select when established Bootstrap conventions and delivery speed fit the brief.

strategies.title: Framework-Neutral Components
strategies.description: Define behavior, tokens, and accessibility independent of a specific UI library.

evidence.summary: Select when portability across implementations is an explicit goal.

strategies.title: Brand Token Constraints
strategies.description: Map approved brand color, type, spacing, imagery, and voice to enforceable tokens.

evidence.summary: Apply when an organization requires consistent branded output.

strategies.title: Purposeful Motion Baseline
strategies.description: Use motion only to explain state, hierarchy, continuity, or feedback.

evidence.summary: Apply as the baseline criterion for every animation decision.

strategies.title: Reduced Motion
strategies.description: Provide meaningful reduced or removed motion behavior without losing state communication.

evidence.summary: Apply to every animated user-facing experience.

strategies.title: Easing System
strategies.description: Define a small set of named easing roles mapped to interaction intent.

evidence.summary: Use to create consistent motion character without arbitrary curves.

strategies.title: Duration Scale
strategies.description: Define bounded duration tokens based on distance, complexity, and interruption cost.

evidence.summary: Use to keep motion timing predictable and reviewable.

strategies.title: Enter and Exit Transitions
strategies.description: Communicate appearance and removal while preserving focus and task continuity.

evidence.summary: Use when elements enter or leave the interaction context.

strategies.title: Shared Layout Motion
strategies.description: Use continuity between related layouts to explain spatial change.

evidence.summary: Use when spatial continuity reduces cognitive effort.

strategies.title: Microinteraction Feedback
strategies.description: Use brief feedback to acknowledge direct user actions and resulting state.

evidence.summary: Use when immediate feedback clarifies action completion or state.

strategies.title: State Change Feedback
strategies.description: Animate meaningful state transitions without delaying the next task.

evidence.summary: Use when state change may otherwise be missed or misunderstood.

strategies.title: Loading and Progress Motion
strategies.description: Communicate waiting and progress honestly without implying false completion.

evidence.summary: Use when operations create perceivable waiting time.

strategies.title: Scroll-Linked Motion
strategies.description: Use scroll-linked changes only when they preserve control, readability, and performance.

evidence.summary: Use sparingly when scroll position meaningfully maps to visual state.

strategies.title: Gesture Motion
strategies.description: Tie gesture response to direct manipulation with clear bounds and recovery.

evidence.summary: Use when direct manipulation is supported and alternatives remain available.

strategies.title: Spatial Navigation Motion
strategies.description: Explain navigation direction and hierarchy through restrained spatial transitions.

evidence.summary: Use when navigation hierarchy benefits from spatial continuity.

strategies.title: Modal Transitions
strategies.description: Introduce and dismiss modal context while preserving focus and reduced-motion behavior.

evidence.summary: Use for modal context changes requiring clear focus management.

strategies.title: List Reorder Motion
strategies.description: Explain additions, removals, sorting, and reordering while preserving item identity.

evidence.summary: Use when list changes would otherwise make item movement hard to follow.

strategies.title: Data Visualization Motion
strategies.description: Use motion to clarify data transitions without distorting values or comparison.

evidence.summary: Use when transition continuity helps interpret changing data.

strategies.title: Skeleton Transition
strategies.description: Transition from placeholder structure to content without layout jumps or false detail.

evidence.summary: Use when known layout structure can reduce perceived disruption.

strategies.title: Interruption-Safe Motion
strategies.description: Allow animations to reverse, finish, or cancel without corrupting interaction state.

evidence.summary: Apply to interactive motion that may be interrupted by users or state changes.

strategies.title: Animation Performance Budget
strategies.description: Set frame, main-thread, asset, and device constraints for motion.

evidence.summary: Apply where animation performance affects supported devices.

strategies.title: Motion Accessibility Review
strategies.description: Review vestibular risk, reduced motion, focus, timing, and user control.

evidence.summary: Apply before approving user-facing motion.

strategies.title: Motion Specification Review
strategies.description: Document trigger, purpose, timing, easing, interruption, and reduced-motion behavior.

evidence.summary: Apply to make motion implementation reviewable and consistent.

strategies.title: Animation Acceptance Testing
strategies.description: Validate motion behavior, interruption, reduced-motion alternatives, and performance.

evidence.summary: Apply before shipping consequential interface motion.
